title: ICARION Physics Configuration
description: Global physics options for collision models and thermalization

enable_space_charge_gpu.description: Request the GPU implementation of the space-charge solver when space_charge_model is auto and CUDA is available

space_charge_model.description: Space charge backend selection. auto uses Direct for small ion counts, Grid for larger ion counts, and may use GPU when enable_space_charge_gpu is true and CUDA is available. direct and grid force the corresponding CPU backend; gpu requires an available CUDA backend.

collision_multi_event_mode.description: Enable approximate multi-collision handling via micro-subcycling within each dt. This is not a full Poisson event-time sampler; use for controlled speed/accuracy studies.

collision_max_events_per_step.description: Legacy name. In collision_multi_event_mode this is the minimum micro-subcycle count per dt, via max(collision_subcycles_per_step, collision_max_events_per_step). It is not a guaranteed upper bound on physical continuous-time collision events.

collision_subcycles_per_step.description: Split each collision application into equal micro-steps and recompute collision rates after each micro-step. collision_multi_event_mode enforces at least collision_max_events_per_step subcycles. This improves collision statistics but does not relax the need for dt_s to resolve fields, gradients, momentum relaxation, walls, and domain boundaries.

ipm_orientation_mode.description: InteractionPotential orientation sampling mode: random picks a new orientation each collision; fixed uses ipm_fixed_orientation_index

ipm_fixed_orientation_index.description: InteractionPotential orientation index used when ipm_orientation_mode=fixed

ipm_vrel_log_prefix.description: If non-empty, enables InteractionPotential relative-velocity histogram logging and writes CSV output to this file/prefix

ipm_momentum_log_prefix.description: If non-empty, enables InteractionPotential momentum diagnostic logging and writes CSV output to this file/prefix

force_ou_for_stochastic.description: Force Ornstein-Uhlenbeck style thermalization for stochastic models
